purpose: Define the repo-source line budget as a reasonable-refactor maintenance signal with advisory default behavior and explicit strict ratchet mode for maintainers and scheduled structure jobs.
machine_boundary: This contract owns line-budget thresholds, near-limit advisory thresholds, reasonable-refactor classification, and reviewed oversized-file baselines for tracked source files. It does not define domain truth, production readiness, release readiness, or quality verdict authority.

reviewed_baselines.reason: characterization_first: current-control provider admission owns runtime/currentness/provider semantics and readback publication authority.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by candidate normalization, transition-request promotion, readback publication, terminal consumption, and fail-closed authority guards after source-of-truth trace.

reviewed_baselines.reason: characterization_first: DomainProgressTransitionRuntime is OPL runtime authority for command/event/outbox, idempotency, replay, read model, and human gate tokens.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Continue extracting runtime parts around command normalization, append/idempotency, fixed-point outcomes, read-model rebuild, replay, and human-gate token lifecycle.

reviewed_baselines.reason: characterization_first: redrive logic mutates runtime queue state and carries currentness, stop-loss, terminal attempt, and provider transport authority.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by redrive decision phase: live-attempt guard, terminal closeout guard, stop-loss policy, retry materialization, and event/readback emission.

reviewed_baselines.reason: characterization_first: skill/plugin discovery and sync is a public OPL/Codex companion boundary with package export and installed-state semantics.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by workspace-root resolution, pack discovery, plugin source registry, sync/apply flow, and JSON readback shaping under existing opl-skills boundary tests.
